title: HERA orchestration evolution mapped to QA orbit dynamics

claims.label: Role-Aware Prompt Evolution = Bateson Learning Levels filtration

bateson_mapping.modulus_change: Level III — not in HERA (would require changing LLM backbone)

witnesses.double_bind: Agent trapped in Level 0 cannot learn from errors requiring Level I+ operators. HERA's credit assignment detects this.

claims.label: Four-phase topology evolution = orbit descent with rebound

phases.name: Initial
phases.description: narrow, shallow, low agent count, minimal cycles

phases.name: Exploration
phases.description: recruits more agents, increasing diameter and cycles

phases.name: Refinement
phases.description: prunes redundant agents, diameter decreases, efficiency rises

phases.name: Optimization
phases.description: compact chains, minimal nodes, high per-agent utility

witnesses.not_singularity: System does NOT converge to single agent (Singularity). Direct baseline (single agent) beaten by 38.69%.
witnesses.qa_interpretation: System finds minimal orbit that solves the task — Satellite, not Singularity

claims.label: Entropy plateau at intermediate level = Satellite convergence

witnesses.observation: After initial rise, entropy stabilizes at intermediate level
witnesses.quote: preventing premature convergence to suboptimal strategies while allowing the integration of novel cooperation pathways

witnesses.hera_finding: Sparse exploration leads to compact, high-performing networks
witnesses.qa_parallel: QA's 81 mod-9 pairs partition into exactly 3 orbits (Cosmos:72, Satellite:8, Singularity:1). Orbit structure IS sparse.
witnesses.efficiency: Node efficiency (F1/|V|) increases over training = more with fewer agents = Satellite more efficient than Cosmos

claims.label: Theorem NT compliance: semantic insights = observer projections

witnesses.firewall: Frozen LLMs prevent observer layer from corrupting generative layer
witnesses.grpo: Group Relative Policy Optimization operates on discrete topology space, conditioned on continuous rewards
witnesses.experience_library: Stores (query_chars, NL_insight, utility_float) — NL and float components are observer projections per T2

witnesses.interpretation: Adaptive topology (orbit-aware routing) >> fixed topology (static role assignment)
witnesses.qa_implication: Static agent roles (current AGENTS.md) lose ~39% vs adaptive orbit-classified routing

qualified_claims.label: Multi-agent coordination does NOT map to RNS/CRT
qualified_claims.detail: HERA's architecture is sequential pipeline, not parallel-modular. CRT analogy is forced.

qualified_claims.label: Agent interaction graph ~ QA coupling matrix is structural, not algebraic
qualified_claims.detail: Transition matrix resembles resonance matrix, but has no algebraic symmetry guaranteed.

falsifiers.description: If HERA converged to a single agent (Singularity), the orbit descent analogy would fail. The paper shows single-agent baseline is beaten by 38.69%.

sources: Li & Ramakrishnan, Experience as a Compass: Multi-agent RAG with Evolving Orchestration and Agent Prompts, arXiv:2604.00901 (2026)
